data.title: Bursa Malaysia Extends Losses At Midday As Decliners Outpace Gainers
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia remained in negative territory at midday, weighed down by broad-based selling as losers sharply outnumbered gainers, reflecting cautious investor sentiment.

At 12:30pm, the benchmark FBM KLCI fell 8.43 points, or 0.48%, to 1,742.42 after trading between 1,741.57 and 1,751.38.

Broader indices also retreated. The FBM ACE Market Index slid 24.36 points to 4,750.77, reflecting weakness in small-cap and growth stocks, while the FBM Emas Index declined 52.14 points, or 0.41%, to 12,753.30. Other key indices mirrored the softer tone. The FBM 70 Index slipped 25.28 points to 17,566.34, while the FBM Shariah Index eased 26.18 points to 12,235.46.

Market breadth was firmly negative, with 639 decliners versus 300 gainers, while 494 counters were unchanged.

Trading activity, however, remained robust, with turnover reaching 1.29 billion shares valued at RM1.13 billion, indicating continued participation despite the cautious mood.

The midday decline suggests investors are locking in recent gains and adopting a wait-and-see stance, with sentiment likely to hinge on fresh catalysts and regional market direction heading into the afternoon session.

data.title: Wall Street Ends Mixed As Cooling Inflation Offsets AI Jitters
data.description:
US stocks ended mixed on Feb 13, with the S&P 500 barely higher after softer inflation data bolstered rate-cut hopes, while the Nasdaq declined as heavyweight technology and communications stocks weakened amid persistent concerns over artificial intelligence disruption.

Reuters reported that the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 48.95 points, or 0.1%, to 49,500.93. The S&P 500 edged up 3.41 points, or 0.05%, to 6,836.17, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 50.48 points, or 0.22%, to 22,546.67.

For the week, all three major indexes posted their steepest declines since November, with the S&P 500 down 1.39%, the Nasdaq sliding 2.1% and the Dow losing 1.23%, reflecting heightened volatility in technology stocks tied to AI competition and elevated capital spending.

Investor sentiment was initially lifted after inflation data showed US consumer prices rose less than expected in January, prompting traders to raise the probability of a June rate cut to 52.3%, according to CME Group’s FedWatch tool. However, technology giants including Nvidia and Apple dragged on the broader market.

Defensive sectors led gains, with utilities climbing 2.69% and real estate rising 1.48%. Healthcare stocks also advanced, as Dexcom jumped 7.6% and Moderna gained 5.3% following strong earnings.

Chip equipment maker Applied Materials surged 8.1% after issuing upbeat revenue and profit forecasts, while networking firm Arista Networks rose 4.8% on strong annual revenue guidance.

In commodities-related stocks, steelmakers including Nucor and Steel Dynamics declined after Peter Navarro of the White House dismissed reports of tariff reductions.

Market breadth was positive, with advancing stocks outnumbering decliners on both the NYSE and Nasdaq, while trading volume totalled 18.61 billion shares, below the 20-day average.

data.title: STI Opens Firmer Near 5,000 Mark
data.description:
Singapore shares opened slightly higher on Thursday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) inching closer to the psychologically significant 5,000 level as banking stocks and key blue chips provided early support in cautious trading.

As at 9.05 am on Feb 12, the STI rose 14.79 points, or 0.3%, to 4,999.37, placing the benchmark index just shy of the milestone level that traders have been watching in recent sessions.

Market breadth was slightly negative, with 76 advancers against 103 decliners, suggesting that gains were concentrated in selected heavyweights rather than broad-based buying across the board. Total trading volume stood at 78.19 million shares with a value of about S$223.30 million in early trade.

Among actively watched counters, DBS rose 0.748% to S$57.93, helping to anchor the index. Other index constituents also traded in positive territory or held steady, including Singtel at S$4.97, UOB at S$39.07 and OCBC Bank at S$21.68. Keppel changed hands at S$12.50, while Wilmar International traded at S$3.59 and Yangzijiang Shipbuilding stood at S$3.41.

Across the broader market, investors continued to keep an eye on regional and global cues, with derivatives trading showing activity in contracts such as the MSCI Singapore Index Futures and major regional index futures, reflecting ongoing positioning by institutional participants.

Meanwhile, sector indices including the iEdge S-REIT Index and the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index were also monitored by investors for signals on real estate investment trusts and mid-cap performance, which often provide clues on risk appetite beyond the blue-chip segment.

For now, the STI’s proximity to the 5,000 level is likely to remain a focal point for traders, with market participants watching whether sustained buying in banks and large-cap counters can push the benchmark decisively above the threshold in the sessions ahead.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Against US Dollar On Steady Domestic Sentiment
data.description:
The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar, despite a firmer greenback, as continued confidence in Malaysia’s economic fundamentals supported the local currency.

At 8 am, the local currency strengthened to 3.9100/9230 versus the greenback from Wednesday’s close of 3.9110/9170.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said encouraging domestic economic data and sustained investor confidence in Malaysia’s macroeconomic outlook continued to underpin local currency demand.

“Strong data points for the Malaysian economy would also keep interest intact in the ringgit. As such, expect the US dollar-ringgit pair to oscillate within a range of RM3.91 to RM3.93 today,” he told Bernama.

However, he added that stronger-than-expected US labour market data had lifted the US Dollar Index (DXY) by 0.13 per cent to 96.920 points, reflecting the resilience of the US economy.

“Therefore, the US Federal Reserve would be inclined to keep the interest rate steady as the inflation rate is likely to stay above the two per cent target.

“As such, traders might be tempted to take some profit due to the US Non-farm Payroll (NFP) releases,” he said.

Mohd Afzanizam noted that the US NFP came in higher than expected at 130,000 in January versus consensus estimates of 66,000, while the US unemployment rate eased to 4.3 per cent from 4.4 per cent, and the US labour force participation rate rose to 62.5 per cent from 62.4 per cent.

At the opening, the ringgit traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

It rose versus the Japanese yen to 2.5514/5600 from 2.5527/5566 at Wednesday’s close, appreciated vis-à-vis the British pound to 5.3266/3443 from 5.3561/3643 yesterday and strengthened against the euro to 4.6431/6586 from 4.6611/6683 previously.

The ringgit went up against the Indonesian rupiah to 232.9/233.7 from 233.0/233.4 at Wednesday’s close, improved versus the Thai baht to 12.5728/6227 from 12.5808/6062 yesterday and was higher vis-à-vis the Singapore dollar at 3.0980/1086 from 3.0990/1040 previously.

It traded almost flat against the Philippine peso, opening at 6.71/6.73 from 6.71/6.72 on Wednesday.

Bernama

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Mixed As Kee Ming Steals The Spotlight With Explosive Debut
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened on Thursday with a mixed tone as the FBMKLCI dipped slightly by 0.05% to 1,755.52 in early trade, while broader market activity showed pockets of strength among high-profile gainers.

The FBM70, which tracks mid-cap stocks, rose 0.21% to 17,594, and the F4GBM index inched up 0.03% to 1,033.92, signalling a cautious start for investors despite selective buying in active counters.

Kee Ming Group Bhd remained the centre of attention on the ACE Market, surging 46 sen or 123% from its IPO price to 84 sen on strong trading volume of 422,280 lots. The company’s initial public offering was oversubscribed 54.16 times, underscoring investor enthusiasm for the industrial products and services player.

Other gainers at the open included HEIM (+1.04 to 24.74), Carlsberg Malaysia (+0.36 to 18.36), and DLADY (+0.22 to 33.22). On the downside, F&N slid 0.14 to 32.16, MPI fell 0.14 to 30.66, and HLFG was down 0.10 to 22.74.

Among the most actively traded counters were TWL (0.025, +0.005), MMAG (0.045), Bornoil (0.010), and Velesto (0.315, +0.005), reflecting broad interest in both newly listed and smaller-cap stocks.

Market participants are closely watching Kee Ming’s debut performance, which has set the tone for early trading sentiment, even as the benchmark index remains subdued. The mixed opening reflects a balancing act between selective buying in high-profile gainers and caution among blue-chip stocks.

As trading progresses, investors are expected to monitor sectoral movements and corporate developments, with particular attention on newly listed companies that have captured strong retail interest.

data.title: Bitcoin Drops Below US$67,000, Ether Falls To US$1,902
data.description:
Bitcoin’s correlation with stocks continued to diverge, with the original cryptocurrency declining while US equities were mostly flat after a stronger-than-anticipated US jobs report, suggesting investor confidence has yet to recover from this year’s crash in digital asset prices.

The largest cryptocurrency dropped below $67,000, before paring the decline during New York trading on Wednesday. Ether, the second-biggest coin, fell as much as 5.3% to an intraday low of $1,902.

Bloomberg

The January jobs data reinforces Federal Reserve officials’ inclination to keep interest rates on hold for now, with traders pushing out their timeline for the next rate cut to July from June. Rate cuts have been historically viewed as positive catalyst for Bitcoin since they generally increase demand for higher returning riskier assets.

data.title: Asian Stocks Soar As Strong US Jobs Data Shakes Rate Cut Bets
data.description:
Asian stocks climbed to record levels on Thursday, propelled by gains in the technology sector, while the dollar strengthened against most major currencies except the yen.

South Korea (.KS11) and Japan (.TOPX, .N225) both reached all-time peaks, helping MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares (.MIAP00000PUS) to another record, up 0.7% and lifting year-to-date gains to around 13%.

Japanese shares have surged following Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s decisive election win over the weekend, with markets expecting increased economic stimulus. The yen rallied 2.7% this week, trading at 153.01 per dollar in afternoon trade, supported by hopes that Takaichi will pursue fiscal responsibility without the need to negotiate with opposition parties.

“Yen shorts are collectively reassessing positions, although at this stage the bearish trend in JPY that started in early 2025 looks more like a reversion to the mean than the start of a structural bull market,” said Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone. “That said, traders need to stay open-minded as the macro picture evolves and where the market decides where it ultimately wants to take JPY.”

Investor attention remains on the US, where a stronger-than-expected jobs report for January has dampened expectations of near-term rate cuts. Job growth accelerated unexpectedly while the unemployment rate fell slightly, signalling labour market stability that could prompt the Federal Reserve to keep rates unchanged.

Thomas Mathews, head of markets for Asia-Pacific at Capital Economics, said, “If labour market conditions are tightening at this point, investors may be overestimating the case for further easing, and Treasuries could be in for a bit more pain.”

Market expectations for a 25-basis-point Fed cut in March had been around 20% before the jobs data but dropped to about 5% after the report, according to CME’s FedWatch Tool. Traders still anticipate at least two rate cuts later in the year. The two-year US Treasury yield jumped 5.8 basis points to 3.505%—its largest single-day gain since late October.

The dollar found support from elevated Treasury yields, rebounding against most currencies. Analysts cautioned, however, that uncertainties around Fed independence and policy risks suggest further positive data will be needed to maintain the recovery.

“Improving global growth prospects and the continued outperformance of non‑US equities keep the case for USD weakness,” OCBC strategists noted.

European futures pointed to a strong opening as investors continue to assess corporate earnings across the region. Market attention is now turning to US inflation data due on Friday, which will be the next key test for expectations of interest rate cuts.

Commodities also reflected geopolitical and market sentiment. Oil prices rose amid concerns over escalating tensions between Iran and the US, with Brent crude up 0.4% at $69.70 per barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude rising 0.46% to $64.93 per barrel. In contrast, spot gold fell 0.44% to $5,058.49 after a more than 1% gain in the previous session.

Reuters

data.title: Ringgit Opens Near Eight-Year High, Strengthens Across The Board
data.description:
The ringgit opened firmer on Feb 13, hovering near its strongest level in almost eight years, supported by persistent weakness in the US dollar and improving sentiment towards Malaysia’s economic outlook.

At 8am, the local currency rose to 3.8945/9110 against the greenback from its close of 3.8995/9060 on Feb 12, approaching levels last seen in April 2018.

The ringgit also strengthened broadly against major currencies, edging up versus the Japanese yen to 2.5494/5604, appreciating against the euro to 4.6224/6420 and gaining against the British pound to 5.3035/3260.

It also advanced against regional peers, improving against the Singapore dollar to 3.0850/0986, rising versus the Thai baht to 12.5257/5885, strengthening against the Indonesian rupiah to 231.4/232.5, and inching higher against the Philippine peso to 6.70/6.73.

data.title: Hang Seng Declines 0.2% On Opening As Investors Stay Cautious On Tech
data.description:
Hong Kong equities opened slightly lower on Thursday, with the benchmark Hang Seng Index declining as investors adopted a cautious stance amid lingering uncertainty over global interest rates, China’s economic outlook and geopolitical developments.

The Hang Seng Index fell 55.82 points, or 0.2%, to 27,210.56 at the opening bell, extending recent volatility as market participants assessed fresh economic data and awaited clearer policy direction from major central banks.

The modest decline reflected cautious investor sentiment following mixed global market performances overnight, as traders balanced signs of economic resilience against concerns over slowing global growth and tightening financial conditions.

Technology stocks, which carry significant weight on the Hang Seng Index, were closely watched as investors evaluated earnings outlooks and demand trends. The sector has remained sensitive to shifts in global technology spending, regulatory developments and interest rate expectations.

Property stocks also remained under scrutiny amid ongoing concerns about China’s real estate sector recovery, which continues to weigh on investor confidence despite recent policy measures aimed at stabilising the market.

Investors are closely monitoring signs of improvement in mainland China’s property market, given its importance to broader economic stability and regional financial markets.

data.title: Heineken Lead Gains, Nestle Slide
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia saw notable movements in selected stocks on Wednesday, with HEIM (3255) leading the top gainers, closing at RM24.40, up RM0.70.

F&N (3689) rose RM0.58 to RM32.88, while KEEMING (0392) added RM0.49 to end at RM0.87. Other gainers included HLIND (3301), which increased RM0.42 to RM18.62, and PETGAS (6033), up RM0.30 at RM18.54.

On the other hand, major losses were recorded by Nestle (4707), which fell RM0.70 to RM110.60, and UTDPLT (2089), down RM0.58 at RM29.62. HLFG (1082) eased RM0.16 to RM22.68, while LYSAGHT (9199) declined RM0.14 to RM2.25. Maybank (1155) also slipped RM0.12 to RM12.14.

Market observers noted that trading volumes were particularly active in mid-cap stocks such as KEEMING and HEIM, reflecting selective investor interest amid a broadly cautious market environment.

data.title: Cautious Trading Keeps Bursa Mixed On Midday, FBM70 Climbs
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the first half of trading today on a mixed session, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) slipping 6.39 points, or 0.36%, to 1,750.00 as at 12.30 pm.

The broader market was similarly varied, with the FBM70 gaining 27.84 points, or 0.16%, to 17,585.13, while the F4GBM eased 2.24 points, or 0.22%, to 1,031.41. The FBMEMAS index declined 27.98 points, or 0.22%, to 12,800.79, and the FBMSHA fell 11.84 points, or 0.10%, to 12,250.59.

In the stock market, Keeming led trading with 64.8 million shares, rising 0.475 to 0.855, followed closely by Zetrix at 0.830. Pharma slipped 0.010 to 0.315, while Borneo Oil fell 0.005 to 0.005. SMRT advanced slightly by 0.005 to 0.165.

Top gainers during the session included HEIM, which rose 0.660 to 24.360, Keeming, Carlsberg (+0.320 to 18.320), F&N (+0.300 to 32.600), and HLIND (+0.220 to 18.420).

Meanwhile, the leading decliners were Nestle (-0.700 to 110.600), UTDPLT (-0.620 to 29.580), HL Bank (-0.120 to 24.680), KLCC (-0.120 to 9.300) and PPB (-0.100 to 11.000).

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Open Lower As Hang Seng Drifts From Recent Highs
data.description:
Hong Kong equities opened on a cautious note on Feb 13, with the benchmark Hang Seng Index declining at the open as investors weighed mixed global cues and sector pressures. Early trading showed the Hang Seng dipping as market participants remained tentative, particularly ahead of key macro data and continued technology sector scrutiny.

The early weakness followed a modest drop in the index in the previous session, with market forecasts pointing to a softer start amid global headwinds and concern over artificial intelligence‑led volatility in tech stocks.

Sentiment is tempered as traders look for fresh catalysts to sustain broader participation beyond recent swings, with heavyweight tech and financial names in focus for direction later in the session.

data.title: Wall Street Hesitates After Payrolls Surprise
data.description:
Wall Street ended largely flat on Wednesday as a stronger-than-expected US jobs report lifted confidence in the economy but dampened expectations for aggressive interest rate cuts, leaving investors reassessing the Federal Reserve’s next move.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 66.74 points, or 0.13%, to 50,121.40. The Nasdaq Composite fell 36.01 points, or 0.16%, to 23,066.47, while the S&P 500 edged down 0.34 points to close at 6,941.47.

Earlier in the session, all three major indexes had traded firmly higher.

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq touched their highest levels in more than a week after January payrolls data showed US job growth far exceeded expectations and the unemployment rate declined to 4.3%.

However, optimism faded as traders trimmed bets on rate cuts.

Although markets still expect at least one 25-basis-point reduction in June, the probability of rates remaining unchanged that month rose to 41% from 24.8%, according to CME Group’s FedWatch tool.

Julia Hermann, global market strategist at New York Life Investments, said investors digested changes to rate cut bets “quite well” because they interpreted the strong jobs report as good news for the economy.

“This is constructive news in that the economy is not in dire need of rate cuts because the jobs market has been showing some new signs of life,” she said.

Julia added, “It comes down to the sweet spot of hiring being strong enough to show us the economy is resilient but not so strong as to derail expectations for future Fed easing.”

Sector performance was mixed. Energy led gains with a 2.6% rise, followed by consumer staples up 1.4%, while financial services and communication services both dropped more than 1%. Chipmakers advanced, lifting the Philadelphia semiconductor index 2.3%, but software stocks slid 2.6%.

Microsoft fell 2.2% and Alphabet declined 2.4%, weighing on the broader market. Robinhood tumbled 8.9% after missing fourth-quarter revenue expectations, while Generac surged 17.9% on strong results.

Investors are now turning their focus to Friday’s Consumer Price Index report for further clues on inflation and the Fed’s policy path.

Reuters

data.title: Nasdaq Sinks 2% As Tech Rout Deepens On AI, Rate Worries
data.description:
Wall Street closed sharply lower on Feb 12, with the Nasdaq sinking 2% as a deepening technology selloff and mounting concerns over artificial intelligence disruption rattled investor confidence ahead of crucial inflation data.

Reuters reported that the Nasdaq Composite fell 2.03% to 22,597.15, while the S&P 500 dropped 1.57% to 6,832.76 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average slid 1.34%, losing 669 points to end at 49,451.98. The selloff accelerated after early gains faded, as investors rotated out of high-growth technology stocks and into defensive sectors such as utilities, consumer staples and real estate.

Technology shares came under heavy pressure after Cisco Systems plunged 12.3%, its worst daily decline since May 2022, following weaker-than-expected margins. The disappointing update triggered broader selling in megacap tech names, including Apple, Nvidia, Broadcom and Amazon, as investors reassessed the sustainability of artificial intelligence (AI)-driven growth.

Investor unease was compounded by concerns over massive AI-related capital spending, with Amazon, Alphabet, Meta and Microsoft expected to collectively invest about US$650 billion this year, raising questions about returns and profit margins.

Transport stocks were also hit hard, with the Dow Jones Transport Average falling 4% amid fears that AI-powered logistics tools could disrupt traditional freight operators. Landstar tumbled 15.6%, while CH Robinson and Expeditors International fell 14.5% and 13.2%, respectively, as investors recalibrated the sector’s outlook.

Adding to the cautious tone, stronger-than-expected labour market data earlier this week dampened expectations for imminent interest rate cuts. Investors are now closely watching the Consumer Price Index report due Feb 13 for clues on the Federal Reserve’s policy path.

With AI optimism giving way to questions over disruption and spending, and inflation data looming, investors are reassessing whether tech’s powerful rally can hold its ground.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Ends Lower, FBM KLCI Slides 0.32%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Wednesday as investors remained cautious amid mixed market signals.

The FBM KLCI (FBMKLCI) slipped 5.54 points, or 0.32%, to 1,750.85, while the broader FBM70 rose 34.33 points, or 0.20%, to 17,591.62.

Other key indices also ended marginally lower, with the F4GBM down 1.87 points (-0.18%) at 1,031.78, FBMEMAS easing 23.33 points (-0.18%) to 12,805.44, and FBMSHA largely unchanged at 12,261.64 (-0.01%).

The session saw active trading in smaller-cap and mid-cap stocks, reflecting selective investor interest.

Among the most active stocks, ZETRIX (0138) closed at 0.820, up 0.005 with 83.6 million shares traded, followed by KEEMING (0392) at 0.870, rising 0.490 on 78.9 million shares. TANCO (2429), PHARMA (7081), and VELESTO (5243) were also heavily traded.

Market analysts noted that sentiment remained cautious as investors weighed corporate earnings against broader economic indicators.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Slightly Higher Ahead Of CNY
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia posted modest gains in early Feb 13 trade, with turnover remaining thin as investors adopted a cautious stance ahead of next week’s Chinese New Year (CNY) break.

At 9.02am, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) edged down 1.28 points to 1,749.57, after opening lower at 1,747.89, from Feb 12’s close of 1,750.85.

Losers outnumbered gainers on the broader market, 251 to 105, while 258 counters were unchanged. Market turnover stood at 169.47 million shares valued at RM99.14 million.

Top gainers included Lysaght Galvanized Steel Bhd increased 13 sen to RM2.38, Ta Ann Holdings Bhd added 11 sen to RM4.63, MISC Bhd up nine sen to RM8.19, AMMB Holdings Bhd grew five sen to RM6.54 and Atlan Holdings Bhd gained four sen to RM2.79, while notable decliners were Nestle (M) Bhd down 50 sen to RM110.10, Malaysin Pacific Industries Bhd declined 22 sen to RM30.78, KLCC decreased 14 sen to RM9.01 and both Hong Leong Bank Bhd and United Plantations Bhd eased 10 sen to RM24.60 and RM29.52 respectively.

Investors remained selective, with light trading activity reflecting caution ahead of the holiday period.

data.title: Bursa Closes Lower As Key Indices Slip, Active Counters Mostly Weaker
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended in negative territory today as key indices retreated amid cautious sentiment.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) fell 11.31 points, or 0.65%, to close at 1,739.54. Broader indices also weakened, with the FBM70 down 21.95 points (-0.12%) to 17,569.67 and the FBM Emas Index sliding 67.24 points (-0.53%) to 12,738.20.

The FBM Shariah Index declined 39.63 points (-0.32%) to 12,222.01, while the F4GBM Index lost 5.56 points (-0.54%) to 1,026.22.

Active counters were mostly lower, led by Zentech and Zetrix in traded volume. Zentech topped the list with 928,379 shares traded, falling 0.5 sen to 0.5 sen.

Zetrix slipped 2 sen to 80 sen with 876,283 shares done, while Hock Soon dropped 4 sen to 56 sen on volume of 641,861 shares.

Tanco bucked the trend, rising 3 sen to RM1.41 with 533,934 shares traded. PharmaNiaga eased 1.5 sen to 30.5 sen on turnover of 416,273 shares.

data.title: UTDPLT, HLIND Lead Top Gainers On Bursa As Nestle, MPI Head Losers
data.description:
Several heavyweight counters led Bursa Malaysia’s top gainers list today, with UTD Plantation and Hong Leong Industries posting the strongest advances.

UTD Plantation rose 56 sen to RM30.18 to top the gainers board, while Hong Leong Industries climbed 36 sen to RM18.98. Quality Concrete added 25 sen to RM1.28, Heineken Malaysia gained 20 sen to RM24.60, and Batu Kawan edged up 14 sen to RM19.74.

On the losing side, consumer and financial heavyweights dominated decliners, led by Nestle and Malaysian Pacific Industries (MPI).

Nestle fell RM1.60 to RM109.00, while MPI slid RM1.22 to RM29.78. Hong Leong Bank dropped 58 sen to RM24.12 and Hong Leong Financial Group shed 58 sen to RM22.10, while Allianz declined 48 sen to RM22.50.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Feb 12, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5 pm on Feb 12, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

data.title: Singapore Stocks Open Lower As STI Retreats From Recent Highs
data.description:
Singapore equities opened lower on Feb 13, with the Straits Times Index (STI) easing 0.81% to 4,976.21, retreating from its recent record above the 5,000‑point mark.

Market sentiment was weighed by profit-taking in heavyweight banking and blue-chip counters, following the benchmark’s historic milestone earlier this week.

Early trading also saw selective weakness across consumer and property-related stocks, offset slightly by gains in tech and mid-cap counters.

Investors are keeping a close eye on market direction after the STI’s recent rally, with cautious positioning ahead of upcoming corporate earnings and regional cues.
